input.content: Calculate the final temperature when 20 kg of water at 30 degree Celsius is mixed with 15 kg of water at 60 degree Celsius.

tools.name: calculate_final_temperature
tools.description: Calculates the final equilibrium temperature after mixing two bodies with different masses and temperatures Note that the provided function is in Python 3 syntax.

mass1.description: The mass of the first body (kg).

temperature1.description: The initial temperature of the first body (Celsius).

mass2.description: The mass of the second body (kg).

temperature2.description: The initial temperature of the second body (Celsius).

specific_heat_capacity.description: The specific heat capacity of the bodies in kJ/kg/K. If not provided, will default to that of water at room temperature, which is 4.2 kJ/kg/K. This is a float type value.

messages.content:

- Before using path-sensitive tools, ground the current working directory and existing file or directory names from prior tool outputs instead of assuming the root directory.
- When emitting tool calls, keep assistant content empty and avoid adding a free-form status summary in the same message.
